value: Cinemachine 3.1.3 has a known bug: swapping CinemachineConfiner2D.BoundingShape2D at runtime doesn't move the camera. Workaround: use multiple CinemachineCameras (one per zone) with priority swapping instead of confiner swapping. CinemachineBrain handles blending between them.

Dash ability (Platformer-d8v) incremental build plan. 5 layers: (1) Basic horizontal dash - Dashing state, OnDash callback, dashSpeed=20, dashDuration=0.12, facingDirection tracking, HandleDash in FixedUpdate skips gravity, IsDashing animator param. (2) 8-directional - GetDashDirection reads moveInput vector normalized, fallback to facingDirection. (3) Charge system - hasDashCharge bool, consumed on dash, restored on landing in HandleCoyoteTime. (4) Wall cancellation - check isTouchingWallLeft/Right vs dash direction, cancel dash on hit. (5) Graduated lockout - dashLockoutTimer, dashLockoutTime=0.15, EndDash preserves 30% momentum, graduated control in ApplyHorizontalMovement same pattern as wall jump. Files: PlayerStateMachine.cs (add Dashing enum), PlayerController.cs (all logic), PlayerAnimator.cs (IsDashing param + skip flip), PlayerStateMachineTests.cs (3 tests). Wire OnDash in Unity Editor.

When closing a bead that mentions specific content types (e.g. 'Hazards (spikes, etc.)'), don't assume the underlying system being implemented satisfies the specific instances. A generic KillZone component is NOT 'spikes' — spikes require a visible prefab with a spike sprite and matching collision shape. Always verify the specific deliverable exists, not just the underlying mechanism. Applies to any bead with parenthetical examples: the examples are the deliverable, not hints about scope.
